stories.title: Expand coordinateInputSchema and wire handleCoordinate to assessPhase (REQ-14)
stories.description: Expands the 16-line stub at server/tools/coordinate.ts. coordinateInputSchema includes: planPath: string (required), phaseId: string (required in v1), masterPlanPath?, coordinateMode?: 'advisory' | 'autonomous' (default 'advisory'), budgetUsd?: number, maxTimeMs?: number, startTimeMs?: number (epoch ms anchor for time budget), currentPlanStartTimeMs?: number, projectPath?, prdContent?, replanningNotes?: ReplanningNote[], haltClearedByHuman?: boolean. Per REQ-14 v1.1 the schema deliberately does NOT expose maxRetries — the cap of 3 is hardcoded in REQ-04 and §7 documents the rationale. Zod schema rejects malformed inputs (wrong types, unknown enum values, negative budgetUsd or maxTimeMs) with a structured MCP error response (isError: true). Calling forge_coordinate with non-existent or unreadable planPath returns a structured error response, not an uncaught exception. handleCoordinate wires the input to assessPhase and returns a serialized PhaseTransitionBrief in the MCP content array. Halt-hard non-latching AC: a halted phase + injected failure → status flips to 'needs-replan' on next call (no stale latch).

acceptanceCriteria.description: coordinateInputSchema declares planPath (required) and phaseId (required) at minimum
Schema declares haltClearedByHuman optional boolean
Schema declares startTimeMs and currentPlanStartTimeMs optional numbers
Schema does NOT expose maxRetries (intentional omission per REQ-14 v1.1 + §7)
Negative budgetUsd is rejected with structured error response (isError: true)
Non-existent planPath returns isError: true (NOT uncaught exception)
Calling forge_coordinate({planPath, phaseId}) on a valid v3.0.0 ExecutionPlan returns a brief without errors
Halt-hard non-latching: halted phase + injected failure on next call → status 'needs-replan' (NOT 'halted')
Tool registered in server/index.ts with description reflecting 4th forge primitive role

stories.title: Implement loadCoordinateConfig with 4-field schema, per-field provenance, and writeRunRecord:false warning chain (REQ-15)
stories.description: Adds loadCoordinateConfig(projectPath) to server/lib/coordinator.ts. Reads .forge/coordinate.config.json with a single fs.readFile call (not walked upward); missing file → empty config (not an error). Schema has exactly four optional fields: storyOrdering ('topological' | 'depth-first' | 'small-first', default 'topological'), phaseBoundaryBehavior ('auto-advance' | 'halt-and-notify' | 'halt-hard', default 'auto-advance'), briefVerbosity ('concise' | 'detailed', default 'concise'), and observability ({logLevel, writeAuditLog, writeRunRecord}). Zod schema uses .strict() at the top level — unknown top-level fields cause a P45 console.error warning naming each unknown field, then fall back to defaults for the whole config (resource-cap fields budgetUsd / maxTimeMs / escalationThresholds named explicitly in the warning since they are MCP input args only). MCP input args override config file fields per-field; brief.configSource records provenance ('file' | 'args' | 'default') for each field. Corrupt JSON, schema-invalid values, EISDIR, and mid-write races degrade gracefully via console.error per P44, never throw. When observability.writeRunRecord === false, the loader emits a P45 warning AND brief.recommendation is prefixed with 'WARNING: crash recovery disabled.' (NFR-C03 opt-out chain). Integration points: storyOrdering reorders topoSort output; phaseBoundaryBehavior branches the brief phase-complete path; briefVerbosity shapes recommendation; observability.* gates console/audit/run-record writes.

acceptanceCriteria.description: loadCoordinateConfig exported from coordinator.ts
Test (a): no config file → defaults applied + empty configSource (or all 'default')
acceptanceCriteria.command: npx vitest run server/lib/coordinator.test.ts -t 'config.*no file|loadCoordinateConfig.*missing' 2>&1 | grep -q 'passed'

acceptanceCriteria.description: Test (b): full config file → all 4 fields applied + configSource shows 'file' for each
acceptanceCriteria.command: npx vitest run server/lib/coordinator.test.ts -t 'config.*full|loadCoordinateConfig.*all fields' 2>&1 | grep -q 'passed'

acceptanceCriteria.description: Test (c): MCP args override some config fields → mixed provenance ('file' for some, 'args' for others)
Test (d): corrupt JSON → graceful fallback to defaults, console.error called, never throws
Test (e): schema-invalid value (e.g. storyOrdering: 'random') → field skipped with warning, valid fields still applied
Test (f): mid-write race (file exists but partially written) → graceful fallback
Test (g) behavioral: depth-first storyOrdering on a 6-story 2-chain plan finishes one chain before crossing
writeRunRecord: false → P45 console.error warning + brief.recommendation prefixed with 'WARNING: crash recovery disabled.'
acceptanceCriteria.command: npx vitest run server/lib/coordinator.test.ts -t 'writeRunRecord.*false|crash recovery disabled' 2>&1 | grep -q 'passed'

acceptanceCriteria.description: Resource-cap field budgetUsd in the config file → rejected by .strict() with named-field warning

stories.title: Advisory-mode checkpoint gates: brief IS the checkpoint (REQ-16)
stories.description: Per REQ-16, in advisory mode (coordinateMode === 'advisory') the brief.status field and recommendation string ARE the complete checkpoint signal. No separate checkpointRequired field is emitted. No separate pause-and-wait call. No coordinator-local gate state. Resuming from a checkpoint is a plain re-invocation of forge_coordinate with the same inputs — no resume token, no stored gate state. Autonomous mode (deferred to v2) may emit checkpointRequired: true; v1 never emits this. Determinism: running forge_coordinate twice in a row with identical inputs produces briefs whose non-timestamp fields are structurally equal (NFR-C02).

acceptanceCriteria.description: Advisory mode brief never emits checkpointRequired field
Resume = plain re-invocation: calling forge_coordinate twice with same inputs returns structurally equal briefs (excluding timestamps)
Coordinator does NOT write any gate state file to disk (verified by directory listing in test)
acceptanceCriteria.command: npx vitest run server/tools/coordinate.test.ts -t 'no gate.*state|stateless.*advisory' 2>&1 | grep -q 'passed'

stories.title: Integration tests including configSource end-to-end and halt-hard 3-step clearing state machine (NFR-C05, NFR-C07, NFR-C10)
stories.description: Test-scaffolding story (no REQ mapping per PRD §11). Adds 17-22 integration tests at server/tools/coordinate.test.ts covering: multi-story dispatch, budget enforcement (including incomplete cost data), time enforcement (including missing startTimeMs), crash recovery, INCONCLUSIVE routed to ready-for-retry, advisory brief, error handling, plan mutation reconciliation, corrupt run records, empty-dependency plans, all-stories-done, retry-exhausted → needs-replan, dep-failed transitive propagation, dep-failed dominates failed, 3-FAIL-then-PASS → done, mixed failed+ready → needs-replan, LAST RETRY substring, two-root dep-failed-chain, failed-replanned → new pending, dep-failed-upstream-replanned → downstream-pending, dangling-dependency. Also: (1) configSource end-to-end fixture with .forge/coordinate.config.json setting storyOrdering: 'depth-first' AND briefVerbosity: 'detailed', call with arg briefVerbosity: 'concise' → asserts brief.configSource shows mixed file/args provenance. (2) Halt-hard 3-step clearing state machine: fixture config phaseBoundaryBehavior: 'halt-hard'. Call 1 at phase completion → status 'halted' + synthetic blocking note. Call 2 without flag → still halted (idempotent). Call 3 with haltClearedByHuman: true → status 'complete', note absent. NFR-C10 byte-identity verified via golden-file comparison on 3+ fixtures (no config file vs empty {} config) excluding configSource.

acceptanceCriteria.description: coordinate.test.ts has at least 17 passing integration tests
configSource end-to-end test asserts mixed file/args provenance
Halt-hard 3-step clearing state machine: call 1 halted, call 2 still halted, call 3 with flag → complete
NFR-C10 golden-file byte-identity: full test suite once with no config and once with empty {} config produces byte-identical brief outputs (excluding configSource)
NFR-C05 Windows compatibility: full coordinator test suite passes on windows-latest CI (verified by CI matrix)
NFR-C01 advisory mode $0 across the full file set
acceptanceCriteria.command: test -z "$(grep -n 'callClaude\|trackedCallClaude' server/lib/coordinator.ts server/lib/topo-sort.ts server/lib/run-reader.ts server/lib/run-record.ts server/validation/execution-plan.ts server/types/coordinate-result.ts server/tools/coordinate.ts 2>/dev/null)" && echo EMPTY-OK | grep -q EMPTY-OK

acceptanceCriteria.description: NFR-C07 schema 3.0.0 compatibility: assessPhase accepts all existing v3.0.0 fixtures from prior phases without mutation
Full test suite green

stories.title: Dogfood forge_coordinate against a multi-story plan and write the dogfood report
stories.description: Test-scaffolding story (no REQ mapping per PRD §11). Runs forge_coordinate against a multi-story execution plan (e.g. an existing forge-generate phase plan or a synthesized 6+ story plan with dependencies and prior RunRecords) and verifies: (1) topological ordering is correct; (2) status classification matches expectations across the 6 states; (3) at least one ready-for-retry path is exercised by injecting a FAIL record; (4) at least one dep-failed propagation is exercised by injecting a 3-FAIL terminal-failed root; (5) configSource is populated; (6) no crashes. Writes the dogfood report at .ai-workspace/plans/forge-coordinate-dogfood-report.md with a per-story status table, a brief.recommendation excerpt, the configSource map, and a 'gaps observed' section listing any spec discrepancies for forge-plan to address before S4.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Dogfood report exists at .ai-workspace/plans/forge-coordinate-dogfood-report.md
Dogfood report contains a per-story status table covering at least 6 stories
Dogfood report includes a brief.recommendation excerpt
Dogfood report includes the configSource map
Dogfood report has a 'Gaps observed' section (may be empty, but the section header exists)
Dogfood run did not crash (forge_coordinate returned a brief, recorded in the report)

stories.title: forge_evaluate coherence mode adds mechanical spec-vs-types vocabulary-drift check (F-03 secondary)
stories.description: Closes the F-03 blindspot surfaced during S3 dogfood: PRD said EvalReport.findings sorted by (failedAcId, description) but the actual type at server/types/eval-report.ts has been criteria: CriterionResult[] with {id, status, evidence} since v0.11.x. Coherence mode currently checks PRD-vs-master-vs-phase wording consistency, NOT PRD-vs-actual-TypeScript-types — vocabulary drift slips through every coherence pass undetected and only surfaces when an implementer (lucky-iris in S3) has to adapt to the type at code time. This story adds a mechanical check to handleCoherenceEval (server/tools/evaluate.ts) that for every backtick-quoted PRD reference of the form `{TypeName}.{fieldName}` (e.g. `EvalReport.criteria`, `RunRecord.metrics`, `Story.dependencies`) looks up {TypeName} in the actual TypeScript interface declarations under server/types/ and verifies {fieldName} exists on the type. Orphan references (field does not exist on the named type) emit a coherence finding with code 'spec-vocabulary-drift' and severity 'major' (or the next available finding code in the coherence handler's existing namespace — lucky-iris's call which numeric/string code to use; the exact code does not matter, the failure mode does). Implementation: new server/lib/spec-vocabulary-check.ts module containing (1) parseTypeDefinitions(typesDir): reads every .ts file under server/types/ and builds a Map<TypeName, Set<FieldName>> using a regex-based extractor (no full TypeScript AST parser needed for interface declarations — interface field syntax is regular enough). Handles `interface X { fieldA: T; fieldB?: U }` and `type X = { ... }` shapes. (2) extractTypeReferences(prdContent): scans the PRD body for backtick-quoted CamelCase.camelCase patterns and returns ReadonlyArray<{type: string, field: string, line: number}>. (3) verifySpecVocabulary(prdPath, typesDir): composes (1) and (2), returns ReadonlyArray<{type, field, line, kind: 'unknown-type' | 'unknown-field'}>. handleCoherenceEval imports verifySpecVocabulary, calls it on the loaded prdContent + 'server/types', maps each result to a coherence finding, and threads the findings into the existing coherence finding aggregator so they show up in the report alongside wording-consistency findings. NFR-C01 preserved: zero LLM calls, all mechanical. Known limitation (documented in JSDoc, not blocking): nested type references (e.g. `RunRecord.metrics.estimatedCostUsd`) only verify the top-level field — verifying nested paths requires walking the type alias graph and is deferred to a follow-up if drift recurs. Type aliases imported via `import { Foo } from './bar'` are handled by reading the imported file's exports (one-hop only, no transitive resolution). Cross-package types (e.g. third-party libraries) are skipped with a debug log, never flagged.

acceptanceCriteria.description: New file server/lib/spec-vocabulary-check.ts exists and exports verifySpecVocabulary
parseTypeDefinitions builds a Map with at least the existing types EvalReport, RunRecord, Story, Phase from server/types/
extractTypeReferences finds backtick-quoted TypeName.fieldName patterns in markdown content
Drift detection POSITIVE: a fixture PRD referencing `EvalReport.findings` (field that does NOT exist on the real type) returns exactly 1 unknown-field result naming EvalReport and findings
Drift detection NEGATIVE: a fixture PRD referencing only valid fields (e.g. `EvalReport.criteria`, `EvalReport.verdict`) returns an empty result array
acceptanceCriteria.command: npx vitest run server/lib/spec-vocabulary-check.test.ts -t 'no drift.*valid fields|negative.*empty' 2>&1 | grep -q 'passed'

acceptanceCriteria.description: Unknown type handling: a fixture PRD referencing `FooBar.baz` (type FooBar does not exist in server/types/) returns kind: 'unknown-type' (NOT a crash, NOT a false unknown-field)

Real PRD regression check: running verifySpecVocabulary on the actual docs/forge-coordinate-prd.md against the actual server/types/ directory returns ZERO unknown-field results (confirms PRD v1.2 vocabulary cleanup is complete and the check would have caught F-03 if it had existed at S3)
acceptanceCriteria.command: npx vitest run server/lib/spec-vocabulary-check.test.ts -t 'real PRD.*zero|forge-coordinate-prd.*no drift' 2>&1 | grep -q 'passed'

acceptanceCriteria.description: handleCoherenceEval calls verifySpecVocabulary and threads results into the coherence finding aggregator
End-to-end coherence test: a fixture PRD with deliberate vocabulary drift causes the coherence report to fail (verdict FAIL or non-empty findings array with the spec-vocabulary-drift category)
NFR-C01 preserved: no callClaude/trackedCallClaude in the new module
tsc --noEmit clean with the new module
Full vitest suite green (existing 397+ tests still pass plus new coverage)
